title: Dev SQLite env governance: gitignore, env templates, README, safe .env.local bootstrap
description: Infra-engineer-owned increment: ensure `.data/` is ignored by Git; align `.env.example` with optional server-only `TODO_SQLITE_PATH`; provide a documented one-command or script path that creates workspace-root `.env.local` with safe dev defaults (empty `TODO_SQLITE_PATH` for default DB path, or an explicitly documented fictitious example path—never real secrets); extend README with environment setup (copy `.env.example` to `.env.local`, Next.js + `bun run dev`) and local SQLite lifecycle (default path per `src/server/todo-sqlite-path.ts`, when the file appears, reset by deleting `.data/` or the configured file, Windows/OneDrive or sync-folder caveats). Do not add or duplicate SQLite adapter, migrations, or `TodoRepository` wiring under infra-only surfaces.
acceptance: `.gitignore` contains a rule that ignores the `.data/` directory (verify no tracked `.data/` content; pattern uses `.data/` as requested).
`.env.example` documents optional `TODO_SQLITE_PATH` with comments stating it is server-only (no `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`, not for client bundles).

A single documented command and/or infra-owned bootstrap script creates `.env.local` at the workspace root with safe dev values: either `TODO_SQLITE_PATH=` (empty) so `getTodoSqliteDatabasePath()` uses the default `<cwd>/.data/todos.sqlite`, or another documented non-secret placeholder path; `.env.local` remains gitignored and is never committed.
README includes an Environment section: copy `.env.example` to `.env.local`, Next.js context, and run `bun run dev` per project conventions.

README includes a Local SQLite database section: default resolved path is `<project-root>/.data/todos.sqlite` when `TODO_SQLITE_PATH` is unset (per `src/server/todo-sqlite-path.ts`), when the DB file is created/opened, how to reset safely (e.g. delete `.data/` or remove the configured file), and a warning about Windows/OneDrive or other sync folders hosting the repo or `.data/`.
No new SQLite driver code, migrations, or duplicate adapter/repository logic is added under infra-only paths; persistence stays in existing backend/infrastructure tasks.
Fresh-clone flow per README plus bootstrap yields a runnable dev setup without requiring commits of `.data/` or `.env.local`.
